title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries · Recovery Case: Gita Submodule Security Credentials
summary: A product pins three component repositories, one URL moved, a fork rewrites a dependency, recursive checkout executes untrusted configuration, and release builds disagree on nested state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita submodule security credentials increment in submodule dependency and trust manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.
Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Submodule Security Credentials
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G82 is a theory in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Submodule Security Credentials contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat urls, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-submodule-security-credentials — Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift
git submodule status --recursive
# expected: prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states
# observation: the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0
# changed-case: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration
# transfer-limit: never execute untrusted nested hooks, builds, or recursive commands outside isolation
